title: Grammar Level 2: SOV Word Order
description: Learn how Hindi sentences are structured. Unlike English (Subject-Verb-Object), Hindi places the Verb at the very end.
keyPoints: English: Subject + Verb + Object (I drink water).
Hindi: Subject + Object + Verb (मैं पानी पीता हूँ).

title: Subject-Object-Verb
explanation: In Hindi, the object comes before the verb. 'पानी' (water) is placed between 'मैं' (I) and 'पीता हूँ' (drink).

prompt: What is the Hindi word for 'Tea'?

englishSentence: I drink water / Tôi uống nước
He drinks tea / Anh ấy uống trà

title: SOV Word Order Mastered
takeaways: Understood that verbs are placed at the end of Hindi sentences.
